title: Pentacalia buchtienii
taxonomy: Pentacalia buchtienii belongs to the family Asteraceae, which is a large and diverse group of flowering plants commonly known as sunflowers, daisies, and asters. Within the family Asteraceae, Pentacalia buchtienii is classified in the subfamily Asteroideae. Further classification places it in the tribe Senecioneae, which is a group of plants characterized by their daisy-like flowers and often found in mountainous regions. At the genus level, Pentacalia buchtienii is a member of the genus Pentacalia, which is a group of flowering plants primarily found in the tropical and subtropical regions of the Americas and the Pacific Islands. Its classification within the kingdom Plantae indicates that it is a type of vascular plant.

intro: Pentacalia buchtienii, also known as the Bolivian Pentacalia, is a flowering plant species in the family Asteraceae. Traditional medicine has utilized Pentacalia buchtienii for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which are said to aid in the treatment of various ailments such as wounds, fever, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
